# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export function middleware(req) {
  const { cookies } = req;
  const token = cookies.get('auth_token');

  if (token) {
    // Verify the token (you can implement your own logic here)
    const isValidToken = verifyToken(token);

    if (isValidToken) {
      return NextResponse.next();
    }
  }

  // If no token or invalid, redirect to login
  const url = req.nextUrl.clone();
  url.pathname = '/login';
  return NextResponse.redirect(url);
}

function verifyToken(token) {
  // Implement your JWT verification logic here
  return token === 'valid_token'; // Replace with real validation
}

// Apply the middleware to specific routes
export const config = {
  matcher: ['/protected/:path*'],
};
